description_long: This object consists of four distinct stones stacked vertically, each with a unique color and texture. The bottom stone is the largest, rounded, and has a tan-brown coloration with a slightly mottled surface. Above it sits a smaller, smooth, light gray stone, followed by a rougher, irregularly shaped brownish stone with visible surface undulations. The topmost stone is the darkest, nearly black, and appears more angular and dense than the others. The stones are closely aligned, forming a balanced column that could serve as a natural barrier or decorative feature in an urban setting. The surfaces show signs of weathering, with varying degrees of roughness and natural patterning.
description: A vertical stack of four differently colored and textured stones, forming a balanced column with natural weathering and surface variation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full vertical stack, highlighting the size gradient from the large tan base to the small dark top stone.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the individual shapes and thicknesses of each stone, emphasizing their distinct profiles and surface textures.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the alignment of the stones, with the largest stone now at the front and the stack's tapering shape visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the stones' arrangement and the differences in their widths and surface details.
attribute_placement: stacked with other stones as a decorative or functional barrier
attribute_pattern: Natural mottling and subtle veining, with irregular surface pitting and color variation across each stone
attribute_weathering: Surfaces are rough and weathered, with some stones appearing smoother from erosion and others showing more pronounced texture
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with other stones or landscaping elements, may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well in green or gravel areas
